free_text: -Complete spectroscopy of V-51,52 via the V-51,52(N,G)
Details of exp.method and calibration procedure.
reaction,used for calibration of high energy part of
High flux reactor at ILL, Grenoble.
The samples were exposed to the thermal neutron
flux of 5.E+14 neutrons/cm2/sec in the H7 in-pile
Two vanadium oxide V2O3 targets,contained in cylinders
of reactor grade graphite, were used. One of them had
a  natural isotopic composition, i.e. 99.75% V-51 and
0.25% V-50, and had a V-51 content of 117 mg.
The other was enriched to 36% in V-50 and had a mass
of 25 mg (6 mg V-50 and 11 mg V-51).
For calibration purposes 16 mg of NaCl was added to
the oxide samples in separate runs.
The gamma spectra were recorded with either a
20% efficient intrinsic Ge detector having 2.2 keV
FWHM resolution at 1.33 MeV, or a pair spectrometer.
The pair spectrometer had a central Ge detector, 80 mm
long and with a square cross section of 15 by 15 mm.
Its energy resolution was 2.3 keV FWHM at 2 MeV and
and efficiency calibrated respect to Cl-36 standard.
Full energy range of Cl-36 lines was sufficient for
In addition, the high energy part of the pair spectrum
was calibrated using the N-14(N,G) reaction. The Ge
detector was used to cover the 50 keV - 3 MeV range,
while the pair spectrometer was used for the 2 to 8
-Neutron binding energy in V-51 was determined to be
11051.112keV with stat error 22 eV, total error 167eV,
